Western Marble company also have
samples Of their respective quarries
in the rough. finished and polished
states.


Ymir's mineral display is not yet
all set up, but E. w. Widdowson,
who is in charge. promises to have
it ready by tomorrow. 1t will en-
brace samples from most of Ymir
camps best known producers.
Buffalo Hump has one solitary rep
resentation. being some splendid
looking quartz from the Atlas
The Occur dAlenes has a huge ex-
hibit Of specimen chunks of argen-
tiferous ores weighing from NO to
2700 pounds. The largest is a sample
from the Mammoth mine. The Tiger
is represented by a 1000-pound speci-
men. other well known mines that
contribute are the Standard, Morning,
Bunker Hill, Hecla and Last Chance
The Hercules mine has a special cab
met containing samples of crystal
ized lead ores.


Republic camp has a poor showing
this year. the only representation be-
ing from the Tom Thumb, Quilp and
Morning Glory mines. Though COM
ing longer distance, Twlsp, in Okla
hogan county. has sent many speci-
mems of it's rich gold-copper ores.


Rossland. the Golden Camp" of
British Columbia, has a 1O70-pound
sample from the Rossland Giant that
typifies the greatness of its lodes of
gold-copper rock. A sample from the
Jumbo mine is interesting, as it
shows tellurides. Other mines repre-
sented are the No. 1, LIE Roi. Velvet.
Nickel Plate, War Eagle and Iron
Mask
